questions.q: What is the term used when a new partner is added to an existing partnership firm?
On the admission of a new partner, the profit sharing ratio of the existing partners will _______.
Explain the concept of revaluation of assets and liabilities on the admission of a new partner.
questions.answer: On the admission of a new partner, the assets and liabilities of the firm are revalued to determine their current values. This is done to ensure that the new partner is not prejudiced and that the existing partners are not benefitted at the expense of the new partner. The increase or decrease in the value of assets and liabilities is adjusted through the partner's capital accounts.

questions.q: State the reason for preparing the revaluation account on the admission of a new partner.
questions.answer: The revaluation account is prepared to record the increase or decrease in the value of assets and liabilities on the admission of a new partner.

questions.q: What is the accounting treatment for the amount of goodwill brought in by a new partner?
questions.answer: It is credited to the capital accounts of the existing partners in the ratio of their sacrifice.

questions.q: On the admission of a new partner, the balance of the general reserve is transferred to _______.
questions.answer: The capital accounts of the existing partners

questions.q: Explain the concept of sacrifice ratio and its significance on the admission of a new partner.
questions.answer: The sacrifice ratio is the ratio in which the existing partners sacrifice their share of profits in favour of the new partner. This ratio is used to calculate the amount of goodwill brought in by the new partner and to adjust the capital accounts of the existing partners.

questions.q: State the effect of admission of a new partner on the financial position of the firm.
questions.answer: The admission of a new partner may result in an increase in the capital of the firm, which can be used to finance business expansion or to repay debts.

questions.q: What is the purpose of preparing the partner's capital accounts on the admission of a new partner?
questions.answer: To adjust the capital of the existing partners and to record the capital introduced by the new partner.

questions.q: On the admission of a new partner, the existing partners are said to have made a _______.
